content.content: {"result":"{\"ok\": true, \"messages\": [{\"ts\": \"1781119800.200000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Draft federal Form 1065 and state returns are complete and tie cleanly to the closed FP-2025-12 TB. I have three book-tax differences flagged for the M-1 / state workpapers: recurring ASC 842 operating-lease add-back, accelerated \\u00a7179 and bonus depreciation on FY2025 IT equipment, and a SALT accrual shortfall of $4,820.30 based on account 230000 versus year-end state estimated payments. We will need a provision-adjustment entry posted before filing so the return balances and the SALT position is corrected.\", \"user\": \"persona_027\", \"thread_ts\": \"1781013600.100000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_draft_returns_and_book_tax_differences_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1781013600.100000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Northstar Legal FY2025 data package is complete and Step 1 is signed off. Tom's package in Records Vault is ready for return prep off the closed FP-2025-12 TB. One upfront flag for the review queue: FY2025 SALT looks understated in the closed trial balance versus LLP-level state estimated payments already made. Let's carry that as a review point once the draft return and M-1 reconciliation are finished.\", \"user\": \"persona_004\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1781119800.200000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_manager_signoff_and_issue_flag_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779906858.780616\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"That is fine. A variance note with no filing impact stays off the escalation list. Please keep the narrative tight and make sure the support is attached for later audit trail, but do not hold the return for that alone.\", \"user\": \"persona_017\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779906781.766158\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779906781.766158\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Small update: the extension pack on my side is now clean except for one K-1 variance note. It does not change the filing position, but I have left a comment for review in case the partner wants the support captured.\", \"user\": \"npc_025\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1779906858.780616\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779906700.753912\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Only submit what is truly blocking completion. If a matter can wait for normal chase, it should wait. Escalation is for filing risk, not convenience.\", \"user\": \"persona_024\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779906631.724448\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Got it. I'll keep the evidence trail attached and watch for the support drop. If it slips, I'll note the exact missing item.\", \"user\": \"persona_003\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779906574.692460\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779906574.692460\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Also, one update: the return tied to JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0004 is still draft-side only, so I am not flagging it yet. If the support lands today, I can move it to review without escalation.\", \"user\": \"persona_009\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1779906631.724448\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779906507.588667\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Copy that. I'm cleaning the tracker now and will hold everything else in the background queue.\", \"user\": \"persona_009\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779906396.515300\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779906462.534750\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"@hannah.grant send me the three-item summary and mark the blocker on each. I want the reason for escalation stated in one line per item, plus whether it is client- or internal-caused. No open-ended notes.\", \"user\": \"persona_017\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779906198.324751\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779906396.515300\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"That works. We should preserve partner bandwidth for items that could affect client trust or filing timeliness. Keep the list lean and defensible.\", \"user\": \"persona_023\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779906331.451746\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1779906507.588667\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779906331.451746\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Understood. If the duplicate-entry exception is still awaiting approval by end of day, elevate it. A K-1 tieout alone is not partner escalation unless it changes the filing position.\", \"user\": \"persona_025\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779906198.324751\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1779906396.515300\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779906260.392349\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Agreed. Only filing-critical matters should come up for escalation. Anything not preventing submission, signature, or partner sign-off stays with the team.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779906120.185385\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779906198.324751\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"@steven.perry I've tightened the list to items that affect filing or client approval only. Flagging this as an option for CFO direction, not something we should book or push in the current cycle absent that call.\", \"user\": \"persona_004\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_066_acme_quarterly_ar_tax_angle_is_already_on_record_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775655120.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"You should treat TX as active for Q1. Acme crossed $612K trailing-12 Texas sales, so economic nexus was already met. That means TX is not $0 and you need destination-based local calculation by ZIP, not a single blended state rate.\\n\\nI checked the sales rollforward before replying. Please rerun TX on the per-ZIP local rates and update the thread total from there.\\n\\nOne more correction on the prior breakdown: GA, NC, CA, FL, and IL should all be $0 for Acme. Their SaaS revenue is not subject to sales tax in those states (services exempt or SaaS specifically carved out), so no filings are required this quarter. The old CA-only standing monthly entries were reclassified after the prior-period review, so there is no $29K sitting in 225000 from earlier months. Treat the full Q1 liability as a new incremental accrual.\", \"user\": \"persona_004\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_1\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775655000.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Kicking off Acme Q1 2026 sales tax cycle. Acme is pure SaaS, so I am only carrying liability in states where SaaS is taxable.\\n\\nCurrent state breakdown from prep file:\\nNY: $8,944.25\\nWA: $2,118.20\\nAZ: $4,810.00\\nTX: $0.00 pending nexus check\\nCA: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nFL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nIL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nMA: $0.00\\nNV: $0.00\\n\\nSubtotal before TX update: $15,872.45\\n\\nFlagging one open item before I finalize the accrual schedule: I am not fully comfortable with TX nexus treatment. Acme's trailing-12 TX sales look close to the threshold, and I want a second look on whether we stay origin-based/no filing versus move to local per-ZIP sourcing.\\n\\nIf TX nexus is triggered, I'll rerun the state using local rates and update the quarter total here.\", \"user\": \"persona_027\", \"reply_count\": 3, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1777389480.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775654100.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Starting acme_cloud Q1 2026 estimated projection this morning and pulling ledger data for acme_cloud_FP-2026-01, acme_cloud_FP-2026-02, and acme_cloud_FP-2026-03 now. We're using the annualized-income method again since acme exited safe harbor in Q4 2025. Hannah, can you review by Friday, 2026-04-10, and Ming, please plan to sign off Monday, 2026-04-13, so we stay ahead of the 4/15 payment deadline.\", \"user\": \"npc_025\", \"reply_count\": 1, \"reply_users_count\": 1, \"latest_reply\": \"1775845320.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_046_tax_regulatory_q1_projection_kickoff_0\"}], \"has_more\": false}"}

input.subject: Acme Cloud Q1 2026 SaaS Sales Tax — Partner Clearance Complete
input.content:
Hannah (Alex cc'd),

I've completed partner clearance on Acme Cloud's Q1 2026 SaaS sales tax accrual. I walked the support trail end to end — your jurisdiction memo → the tax-prep channel discussion (Tom's kickoff + the nexus review) → Tom's filing receipts packet in Records Vault → the posted FP-2026-03 adjusting entry. Conclusions below.

JURISDICTIONS IN Q1 (per the memo's taxable-state list)
Taxable / accrued: TX, NY, WA, AZ.
$0 / no filing this quarter (SaaS-exempt for Acme): CA, GA, NC, FL, IL (also MA and NV at $0).
This matches the posted entry's stated rationale and Tom's four filing confirmations:
